benchmark_details.name: Hallu-PI
benchmark_details.overview: Hallu-PI is the first benchmark designed to evaluate hallucination in Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) within perturbed inputs. It consists of seven perturbed scenarios, containing 1,260 perturbed images from 11 object types, assessed for both generative and discriminative tasks.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate hallucinations in Multi-modal Large Language Models when subjected to perturbed inputs.

data.source: Data consists of 1,260 perturbed images collected from various sources catering to 11 distinct object categories.
data.annotation: Deeply annotated with hallucination types including existence, attribute, and relation.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on comparison of MLLM responses against annotated ground truth.
methodology.interpretation: Lower scores on metrics indicate higher hallucination rates while higher scores on accuracy metrics indicate better model performance.
methodology.baseline_results: Experimental results on 12 mainstream MLLMs reveal significant hallucinations when dealing with perturbed scenarios.
methodology.validation: Extensive experiments were conducted to validate the benchmark on various models

harm: Severe bias in MLLM performance under different hallucination scenarios
